head.hi: General Bandholtz to the Commission to Negotiate Peace

div.p: 207. Polk from Bandholtz. Replying to your 105.Ante, p. 722. The facts did not justify great concern over reported arrests. Horthy’s conduct throughout has been excellent. Present indications are that Friedrich within 24 hours will be replaced by Apponyi or somebody else. Political outlook now encouraging. Reports of Roumanian progress in evacuation not so good. They have just seized entire Hungarian tobacco supply at Szolnok and it is reported excesses are being committed. They have left no liaison officer but one of my officers will be at their headquarters on 19th and report on conditions. Colonel Loree should arrive Paris Thursday with memorandum and so you can question him on all details prior to your departure.
